title: pros and cons about going to fsu - the truth you need to hear

title: pros and cons about going to fsu - the truth you need to hear
body: I am currently deciding between a few schools  and just want to say your video was so good and informative! Definitely interested in study abroad and internships, however I really wanted more diversity so I appreciate you sharing how it is on the campus.

title: pros and cons about going to fsu - the truth you need to hear
body: I’m FSU alumni and 100% agree with with your opinions on FSU/ Tally. It worth mentioning that College of engineering is separated from main campus.

title: pros and cons about going to fsu - the truth you need to hear
body: You're so pretty! I am going to be a senior at FSU and we should totally get in touch <3
author: @meredithashley2591

title: pros and cons about going to fsu - the truth you need to hear
body: yes rent is so affordable (for the most part)! some of the newer complexes are expensive but my boyfriend lives in a 1x1 and pays $700 a month which is crazy! i think personally my biggest con is that the school admits so many people, but most of their majors are limited access. for example even if you apply and are admitted as a nursing major, you still have to get into the program. so many people that i know end up having to change their majors or transfer because its so competitive to be admitted into your major
author: @katieallen3142

title: pros and cons about going to fsu - the truth you need to hear
body: girlllll when I tell you everything you said is true, especially about the diversity issue. I’ve lived in Tallahassee all my life, and I’ve also been apart of a summer camp there for 3 years, so I’m kind of expected to go to FSU. But there is literally no diversity at the school, and I’ve been all around campus. There’s also SO MUCH WALKING, and most buildings look the exact same so it’s really confusing.
author: @karensa8814

title: pros and cons about going to fsu - the truth you need to hear
body: Thanks for mentioning the diversity! I am very interested in FSU (I am from NJ) but diversity is really important to me and I greatly prefer being with a variety of cultures since I love having as many perspectives as possible!
author: @GlamourCat1920

title: pros and cons about going to fsu - the truth you need to hear
body: Good points. I went to fsu for first 2 years but didnt find the academics a priority. Transferred to UF and got a Master in Ed.
author: @drdoc7508

title: pros and cons about going to fsu - the truth you need to hear
body:
We have the same pros and cons and I’m from South Florida too. The diversity issue bothers me too, I didn’t feel so welcomed at orientation but what makes me love FSU is the academics for sure. I’m starting next week, I’m nervous!
I would like to know was the workload of college hard for you? And how do you prepare for midterms and finals?
author: @fabulousideas3848

author: @christianamichael4578

title: pros and cons about going to fsu - the truth you need to hear
body: Hi, great video! I am thinking of studying in the United States as a foreign exchange student, I'm a resident of Denmark. So since I would know absolutely nobody coming there, I wanted to ask about how easy it is making friends on the campus. Is it just a big classroom where nobody talks to anybody and people go straight to their next class, or is it more welcoming in that aspect? Thanks for the great video! :)
author: @mr1dk321

title: pros and cons about going to fsu - the truth you need to hear
body: I minor'ed in Int'l Affairs.  LOVED my 4 years at FSU.  In terms of campus life, I had no problems with it although my dorm (which is apparently no longer in existence) was absolutely horrible.  It was Smith Hall, and we had communal showers.  But my sophomore year, I upgraded to Gilchrist which was fantastic.  There's not much to do in Tallahassee unfortunately.  I think Gainesville is a better town (but don't worry, I hate UF) lol I wish I did Gradschool so I could've stayed longer
author: @PrinceofMacedonVlogs

title: pros and cons about going to fsu - the truth you need to hear
body: Hi, I’m going to FSU in the Fall. Idk what to do about transportation though. My Dad is willing to get me a small motorcycle (and it’s low key my dream to get one lol). I’m debating on getting one or just seeing if I can walk everywhere. Would you say it is convenient to have 2 wheels to get around?
author: @JaneDoe-dm9nx

title: FAMU - Florida A & M University Full Tour
body:
Great virtual tour of my Alma Mater. I would love to see a tour of our College of Law, located in Orlando. Thanks for sharing.

Also, I know it's tempting to say FAMmoo. However, the correct pronunciation of F-A-M-U is FAM-U.
author: @Slayitloud

title: FAMU - Florida A & M University Full Tour
body: It’s good to know that it’s evolved since my days as a student at FAMU. Much of what this video presents are new offerings from my days as a student at FAMU.
author: @RDgoff89

title: FAMU - Florida A & M University Full Tour
body: Good presentation, with only a few corrections.  The building called James Hall by Florida Streaming is actually Jones Hall, where many science classes are taught.  FAMU does have a law school, however it is located in Orlando, Florida.  Bragg Stadium now has a capacity of 22,500 due to renovations for ADA compliance.
author: @jmanhope1745

title: Prairie View A&M University 💜💛 #shorts #pvamu
body:
Y'all have great chemistry. I like this dynamic. However, Z comedy that I saw placated to racial stereotypes and to a crowd that I feel laughs at us and not with us.

If her comedy was more reflective of her being herself like on this episode I think it would actually be dope.
author: @Hamptonlantavegas

title: ProGlide Razor Review from FAMU -- ProGlide Challenge
body: This was a great video! It took me back to my college days at FAMU. I am totally telling my dad and boyfriend to use the Gillette ProGlide! Great marketing guys!
author: @djramsay05

title: ProGlide Razor Review from FAMU -- ProGlide Challenge
body: @TheFXTester Yes, Florida Agricultural & Mechanical University (FAMU) is one of the world's best Historically Black Colleges & Universities (HBCUs) While majority of the students may be African-American, diversity is alive and well at this HBCU.  And no, I'm not a spokesperson for FAMU, I am a product, a due paying alumni and a supporter of this illustrious institution! Go Rattlers!
author: @famupeach28

title: Florida A&M University Gospel Choir - Let Everything That Hath Breath (Psalm 150)
body:
Sopranos:
Let everything that hath breath praise the Lord.

Sopranos/Altos: 
Let everything that hath breath praise the Lord.

All: 
Let everything that hath breath praise the Lord,
for His mighty acts and His wondrous works;
praise the Lord, praise the Lord, praise the Lord.


VERSE 1:
Sopranos: We give Thee honor...
Tenors: oh Lord, we give Thee praise...
Altos: power and glory...

Sopranos: We give Thee honor...
Tenors: oh Lord, we give Thee praise...
Altos: power and glory...

Sopranos: We give Thee honor...
Tenors: oh Lord, we give Thee praise...
Altos: power and glory...

Sopranos: We give Thee honor...
Tenors: oh Lord, we give Thee praise...
Altos: power and glory...

VERSE 2:
Everlasting Father, 
Hosanna, the Prince of Peace.
Alpha and Omega,
Beginning and the Ending.

Everlasting Father, 
Hosanna, the Prince of Peace.
Alpha and Omega,
Beginning and the Ending.

Everlasting Father, 
Hosanna, the Prince of Peace.
Alpha and Omega,
Beginning and the Ending.

Sopranos: Oh praise...
Altos: oh praise...
Tenors: oh praise...

Sopranos: the Lord...
Altos: the Lord...
Tenors: the Lord...

VAMP 1:
Sopranos: A, a, Amen...
Altos /Tenors: Amen.

Sopranos: Ah, ah, Amen...
Altos /Tenors: Amen.

Sopranos: Ah, ah...
All: Amen.

VAMP 2:
Sopranos: Ooh, ooh, ooh, ooh...
Altos /Tenors: ooh.

Sopranos: Ooh, ooh, ooh, ooh...
Altos /Tenors: ooh.

Sopranos: Ooh, ooh, ooh,
All: ooh.

VAMP 1

ENDING:
Amen, Amen,
Amen, Amen, Amen,
Amen, Amen.
author: @ChiazoVelma

title: Florida A&M University Gospel Choir - Let Everything That Hath Breath (Psalm 150)
body: I am a true Wildcat straight from the Halls of Bethune-Cookman. FAMU is like the first cousin with whom you don’t get along. But we love each other. I want to say to these young men and women, I felt like the Lord opened the doors of heaven just so the angels could listen to this beautiful arrangement of praise and worship. Job well done FAMU!!!

body: its good to see a brotha dressed for success. now, this is what our ancestors paved the way for. do your thang young man. take care and be in good health. Dr. A

title: A Day in the Life of a Pharmacy Student
body: I turned my life around 3 years ago! I was strung out. I came from a crappy family. Didn't have a GED. But here I am now, on my way to PharmD school! I cannot wait!!! Thanks for this video! Makes my fears turn into excitement.
author: @justjulie5386

title: A Day in the Life of a Pharmacy Student
body: my bro, you were a huge inspiration to me when I first watched this video five years ago. Since then I went to pharmacy school and got registered. Thank you, still love this video reminds me of pharmacy school.
author: @delanmahlagwai9305

title: A Day in the Life of a Pharmacy Student
body: This was probably the best video I've seen on youtube. This has inspired me to fulfill my dream of attending famu and majoring in pharmacy. I really appreciated your time and effort in making this video because believe it or not, you have, you are, and you will continue to change lives for the better - physically and emotionally. #famu18

title: A Day in the Life of a Pharmacy Student
body: LMAO, "The math started looking like the Matrix." Chemical Engineer trying to become a pharmaceutical engineer, I know what you're talking about!

body: Good luck man I'm about to enter pharmacy school myself, been working in a pharmacy for 8 years and love it. Everyone keep your dreams alive.

title: A Day in the Life of a Pharmacy Student
body: Very accurate information. Pharmacy is not easy, but definitely doable. I'm about to finish up my P3 year, and this video is definitely encouraging. Thanks for uploading!
author: @amyhoang8468

title: A Day in the Life of a Pharmacy Student
body: Wow, it's been such a long time since I've watched this. I watched it before I applied to pharmacy school, now I'm close to graduation ! Good video :)
author: @PrincessAndHenry

title: A Day in the Life of a Pharmacy Student
body: I would say i was really inspired by this video. Im in my 3rd year studying B.pharm in SV college of pharmacy in india and im from Africa. Sometimes i wanted to give up on pharmacy but something tells me not to ever give up. Pharmacy is a very lucrative course that need lots of sacrifices. With this video i just watched, i will never give up on pharmacy. I will strive till d end. U just inspired me with this. Thanks and God bless u real good
author: @dfamous1977

title: A Day in the Life of a Pharmacy Student
body: Such an inspiration! When Mr. Burns stated he was 23 years old my jaw just dropped. I mean Jesus Christ the man is a role model not only for young black American males in the community but all young men everywhere! I sure do applaud your intelligence Mr. Burns! I may only still be in high school and I may be a girl but I'm following YOUR footsteps!
author: @ndie.giselle

title: A Day in the Life of a Pharmacy Student
body: I feel you, I'm currently a 5th year pharmacy student at Rutgers and I decided to be a Pharmacist my 12th grade year too.  Doing track and pharmacy is demanding but putting the work in and getting through the days (and nights) has really taught me about perseverance :) good luck on your NAPLEX
author: @nnenne4

title: A Day in the Life of a Pharmacy Student
body: Thanks a lot for uploading this video, it really has inspired me to become a pharmacist. At first I was having mixed emotions on what I wanted to do after high school, wether it was trade school or College. But then I finally realized that this career has met a lot of my expectations, plus I love helping other people. Once again thanks a lot and hope all is well with you.
author: @MrMarmula

title: A Day in the Life of a Pharmacy Student
body: I am currently in college and I'm taking up Pharmacy. This video was a big inspiration to me because I had the mindset that getting my degree was going to be hell. But you made me realize that all  I have to do is stay focuse and study harder. Thanks!! Oh by the way nice smile. You're so gorgeous;)
author: @mzkoko17

title: A Day in the Life of a Pharmacy Student
body: Great video! I would've gone to FAMU if they'd have mailed out my application the first 3 times I called! At FAMU whites and others are considered minorites at an HBCU so they get preference. So talk what you know! The course work did seem to be 1st or 2nd year material, looked like med chem...but my hats off to him! He has found a way to balance his social life and education, and many of the dry pharmacy students I went to school with could not! Best of luck to you! Do a residency!
author: @jinxxy06

title: A Day in the Life of a Pharmacy Student
body: Saw this video a long time ago when I was still enlisted in the Army and worried I was too old to go back to college. Bam! I start medical school in Texas in July.

title: FAMU Marching 100 GOES CRAZY AGAIN
body: Im trying to figure out why all these differnt people think we dont have any culture. Who else can be the the main attraction at a football game and not be the football team?
